name: Council Governance
description: Defines the authority of the leading clans within a ganasangha.
option_description: Leading clans govern through a republican council rather than a hereditary royal court.
note: This is a gameplay adapter for attested ganasangha republican forms and does not reconstruct a specific constitution or membership roll.

name: Civic Cult Practice
description: Defines the relationship between Rome's public cult and locally observed religious practices.
option_description: Roman public rites coexist with many locally observed cults under imperial order.
note: This is a technical tolerance adapter for plural cult practice; it is not a claim of modern religious freedom or a denial of particular coercions and exclusions.

name: Unfree Status
description: Defines the legal permission for enslavement and manumission within the Roman legal order.
option_description: The Roman legal order permits enslavement while retaining multiple pathways to manumission.
note: This records a legal-status baseline only; it does not quantify enslavement or manumission or reduce diverse provincial practices to one uniform experience.

name: Royal Canal Endowments
description: Defines the court's support for irrigation works and monastic grants.
option_description: Royal patronage supports canals and the communities connected to them.
note: This is a gameplay adapter for attested royal canal endowments and does not reconstruct a general Anuradhapura water-law code.

name: Civic Status
description: Defines the relationship between imperial citizenship and subject communities.
option_description: Provincial communities retain differentiated civic status before the universal grant of 212.
note: The later Constitutio Antoniniana is reserved for its AD 212 history path.

name: Military Recruitment
description: Defines the balance between regular troops and broader levies.
option_description: The Roman field force is centred on long-service regular formations.
note: This is a pre-M7 administrative adapter; exact unit rosters and levy composition remain M7 work.

name: Commandery Administration
description: Commanderies and centrally appointed officials structure the Han state.
option_description: The court governs through commandery and county administration.
note: The estate preferences are technical UI adapters rather than a social classification of Han officeholders.

name: Noble Autonomy
description: Defines the contractual balance between the Arsacid king and powerful noble houses.
option_description: The Arsacid court governs through an accommodation with its great houses.
note: This legal adapter represents the political relationship rather than a reconstructed written statute.

name: Client Governance
description: Defines the balance between a local royal court and its imperial patron.
option_description: The client king preserves local authority while relying on Roman recognition and support.
note: This is a political adapter for Roman client relationships and not a claim that Mauretania and Nabataea used one written constitution.

name: Kushite Royal Authority
description: Defines the court-centred authority of the Kushite rulers.
option_description: Royal authority is maintained through the Kushite court and its religious legitimacy.
note: This is a gameplay adapter for royal authority and does not reconstruct a Kushite legal code.

name: Confederation Governance
description: Defines the balance between the chanyu and the constituent Xiongnu clans.
option_description: The chanyu rules through a confederation whose clans retain substantial leverage.
note: This legal adapter represents confederation politics rather than a written Xiongnu statute.

name: Early Korean Kingship
description: Defines the royal and leading-house balance in Goguryeo.
option_description: The king governs through a developing royal court and leading houses.
note: This is a conservative adapter pending a dedicated early Korean institutional pass.

name: Tribal Governance
description: Defines the relationship between a king and the leading kin groups.
option_description: The ruler governs with the consent and influence of leading kin groups.
note: This is a shared technical adapter and not a claim that Marcomannic and Catuvellaunian institutions were identical.

name: Sub-King Governance
description: Defines the balance between a regional Iranian court and the Arsacid political order.
option_description: A regional king governs through local elites while retaining an Arsacid-facing political relationship.
note: This is a gameplay adapter for a regional court and not a reconstructed Parthian legal text.

name: Buffer-Kingdom Governance
description: Defines the courtly balance needed by a frontier kingdom between larger powers.
option_description: The frontier court preserves internal authority while navigating competing imperial interests.
note: This technical law represents borderland politics without claiming a common written constitution for Armenia and Osroene.

name: Temple Administration
description: Defines the relationship between the Herodian court and the Jerusalem priesthood.
option_description: The Temple's priesthood remains a major civic and religious institution under the Herodian ethnarch.
note: The administrative-law surface is a gameplay adapter and does not reconstruct the exact legal authority of the high-priesthood.

name: Augustan Coinage
description: Defines the imperial monetary standard of gold and silver coinage.
option_description: Gold aurei and silver denarii provide the imperial monetary standard.
note: Engine coinage terms approximate a mixed Roman circulation; denomination ratios and mint jurisdictions are not reconstructed.

name: Wuzhu Coinage
description: Defines the standard copper cash used by the Western Han state.
option_description: Cast Wuzhu cash remains the Han monetary standard at the AD 1 start.
note: The coinage modifiers reuse the installed copper-coin contract and do not claim an exact Han fiscal model.
